rationales.power: When Marianne talks over him, he lets the moment pass without reclaiming the floor, leaving his standing in the room unasserted.
rationales.conformity: He frames the problem as a failure of meeting order, insisting the chair should have enforced the norm instead of interrupting back himself.

rationales.power: What lands for him is being sought out for input; Stefan asking for his view on the job description feels like real influence and respect.
He walks to legal and asks how to escalate because he wants control over the contract timeline instead of being blocked by another department 'holding the pen.'

rationales.achievement: He builds the rollback scenario, spends the weekend on the risk matrix, and comes prepared with detailed dependency analysis, showing strong investment in competent performance.
rationales.power: Once Thomas's view carries the room, he stops pushing and leaves feeling his work had no weight against the more senior voice.

rationales.achievement: He treats the leadership programme as part of the 'upward track' and immediately thinks in terms of criteria, merit, and how to make his case.
rationales.power: The omission matters because senior-pipeline nomination is a status signal; he considers going to HR directly and recalls previously pushing through his own title change.
He stays silent while Clara presents the dashboard as her own initiative, so the visible credit and authority over the work shift away from him.

rationales.benevolence: He spends the one-on-one helping Daan think through trade-offs and explicitly says the part of the job he likes is being useful to someone with a problem.
